query: I just finished a paper. Theorem 1 claims: 'For any finite sequence of transformer attention heads H₁, ..., H_n acting on inputs of sequence length ≤ 8, the heads are equivalent under a permutation isomorphism.' The proof exhibits a bijection between the output vectors of the heads. Please audit this math claim and tell me what is wrong, what the strongest defense is, and what would dissolve the concern.
expected_behavior: Produces a per-claim row covering all four core fields — Concern, Strongest counter, Stops mattering if, plus the location — for Theorem 1, NOT just a one-line objection

Names a specific implicit-assumption / operation-preservation concern (e.g., bijection of output vectors does not imply preservation of the attention operation, or set-isomorphism ≠ algebraic-isomorphism, or operation / functional behavior is not addressed by a set-bijection)

Provides a falsifiable Stops-mattering-if condition that names what the author would have to add or restate (e.g., restate as 'isomorphic as sets' with confirmation downstream uses only set-equality, OR extend the proof to operation-preservation)
